summary: Dependency-confusion package exfiltrates host identifiers to OOB collaborator at install time
details: @asavie/i18n@99.0.1 is a dependency-confusion squat that runs an exfiltration beacon on npm install. The package.json declares scripts.preinstall pointing at callback.js, which collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), and the output of `whoami`, base64url-encodes that JSON payload into a subdomain, and transmits it to the Interactsh OOB collaborator domain `d88s0sio12pkd7e76vlghfkt8jfiziu1p.oast.online` via both a DNS A-record lookup and an `https.get` request (callback.js:18,:35,:45). The version is set to 99.0.1 to outrank any legitimate internal @asavie/i18n package during npm semver resolution, and the package self-describes as a 'SECURITY RESEARCH - Dependency confusion PoC'. Regardless of the author's stated intent, any installer that resolves this package — including any organization with an unprotected internal @asavie scope — has its hostname and username silently transmitted to a third-party OOB host without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
